summary: Comprehensive product-ideal blueprint for a React Flow graph viewer/editor over Agentish workspaces. Excellent causal chains for interaction, editing, trust, and conflict flows. The completeness-trust model (hidden context portals, derived edges, diff layers) is a genuinely well-articulated novel concept. However, significant semantic repetition exists: derived-edge semantics, hidden-context portal semantics, and layer semantics are expressed once through when() chains and then restated nearly verbatim in dedicated prose definition sections, roughly doubling token cost without proportional activation gain. The usesFiles section listing 25+ specific file paths is implementation-resolved content that will drift and belongs in a companion artifact rather than the product ideal.
strengths: Causal chains for user interaction, editing validation, trust interruption, and conflict resolution are clear and complete — a reader can trace any user action to its system consequence

The completeness-trust model (visible layers as partial views not partial truth, hidden-context portals, derived-edge inspectability) is a coherent and novel product concept expressed with low interpretive slack
Strong local recoverability — any section can be read independently with full comprehension of its scope
Vocabulary is well-disciplined: derived edge, hidden context portal, layer, workspace plane, and revision are all defined precisely and used consistently throughout
The edit operation matrix (allows, validates, blocks, maps, executes) covers the full lifecycle without gaps and makes the safety model explicit
weaknesses: Substantial repetition between when() chains and dedicated prose sections — derived-edge semantics, hidden-context portal semantics, and layer semantics are each expressed twice with near-identical content, reducing net semantic density

The usesFiles block (25+ specific paths) and package dependency declarations are implementation-resolved content that will drift independently of the product ideal, creating maintenance burden and layer bleed

Several definesXxxSemantics prose blocks restate constraints already captured structurally, adding token cost without new activation — e.g. the layer semantics prose restates what cloneLayer, refineLayer, and arrangeLayer when-chains already establish

The file's total length (~400 lines of content) makes it harder to hold in working memory as a single product specification; the implementation-structure sections could be extracted without harming the product ideal
